text.title: php-laravel-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-laravel-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Eloquent calls inside Controller methods: User::where(...)->get(), Order::find($id), $model->save() directly in a controller action.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Business logic in the controller cannot be tested without the HTTP kernel. When the query logic changes, it must be found and changed in every controller that duplicates it.

failure-mode.description: Validation via $request->validate() or Validator::make() inside a controller method instead of a FormRequest class.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline validation duplicates rules across methods, makes authorize() logic invisible, and cannot be unit-tested without a real Request object.

failure-mode.description: UserRepository wrapping Eloquent::query() without a second backing implementation (e.g., no ElasticSearch or Redis projection).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Eloquent is already a repository. A wrapper class that delegates to User::query() adds a layer of indirection with no benefit and confuses schema changes (two classes must change instead of one).

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Services called from queue workers or CLI commands have no HTTP request context. request() returns null or a partial request and the service crashes silently.

failure-mode.description: Magic facades (Cache::, Mail::, Notification::) inside Service classes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Facades are hard to mock without facade swap boilerplate. Injected contracts (Mailer, CacheRepository) swap cleanly in unit tests and express the dependency explicitly.

failure-mode.description: DB::transaction wrapping external HTTP calls (Stripe::charge, Http::post, Mail::send).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Locks are held for the full duration of the network call. A Stripe timeout at 5s holds a DB row locked for 5s, potentially deadlocking concurrent requests on the same row.

failure-mode.description: firstOrCreate / updateOrCreate without explicit lockForUpdate under concurrent load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two concurrent requests both miss the existing row, both attempt insert, and the second hits a unique constraint violation. Use upsert() or wrap in DB::transaction + lockForUpdate().

failure-mode.description: Resource accessing relations not eager-loaded by the caller: $this->profile->name inside toArray() without with('profile') in the controller.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lazy-loads one query per model item in a paginated list. A page of 20 users triggers 20 additional queries. Telescope flags it; CI passes because tests run with a single model.
